reports.title: 2026q1-alphabet-earnings-release.pdf
reports.summary: Alphabet delivered a strong Q1 2026 with consolidated revenues up 22% to $109.9B (19% constant currency), marking the 11th consecutive double-digit growth quarter. Google Cloud revenue surged 63% to $20.0B, with backlog nearly doubling to $460B. Operating margin expanded 2pp to 36.1%. Operating cash flow rose to $45.8B and free cash flow reached $10.1B for the quarter.
reports.overview: Revenue growth was broad-based: Google Services +16% (Search +19%, YouTube ads +11%, subscriptions+19%), Cloud acceleration fueled by enterprise AI solutions. Operating income grew 30% to $39.7B. Non-GAAP constant currency growth of 19% shows strong underlying demand. Cloud backlog indicates sustained future revenue visibility. Capital expenditures remained high at $35.7B in Q1, reflecting heavy AI infrastructure investment.
reports.further_direction: Alphabet is well-positioned to capitalize on AI monetization across Search, Cloud, and subscriptions. Continued investment in AI infrastructure and enterprise solutions will drive future growth. Key risks include competitive pressure in cloud and search, regulatory challenges, and potential macroeconomic headwinds. No explicit forward guidance was provided, but strong backlog and operational leverage suggest positive trajectory.

reports.title: Alphabet-Q1-2026-Earnings-Slides.pdf
reports.summary: Alphabet's Q1 2026 earnings show strong Cloud revenue growth of 63% Y/Y and operating margin expansion from 33.9% to 36.1%. Free cash flow remains robust, though forward-looking statements note risks and uncertainties.
reports.overview: Revenue growth driven by Cloud (+63% Y/Y) and Search & Other. Operating margin improved to 36.1% from 33.9%. Cost of revenues increased (TAC +8%, other +24%). Free cash flow TTM positive, with capital expenditures considered. Cautionary language on forward-looking statements and risk factors.
reports.further_direction: Monitor risk factors from 10-K/10-Q, sustain Cloud momentum, manage cost increases, and watch for revenue guidance updates. Continued investment in growth areas like Cloud and AI.

reports.title: 2025q1-alphabet-earnings-release.pdf
reports.summary: Alphabet Q1 2025 revenues increased 12% (14% constant currency) to $90.2B, with Google Services up 10% to $77.3B and Google Cloud up 28% to $12.3B. Operating income rose 20% with margin expanding 2pp to 34%. Net income grew 46% and diluted EPS improved 49% to $2.81. Free cash flow was $17.6B for the quarter.
reports.overview: Strong performance across search, YouTube, subscriptions, and cloud driven by AI infrastructure and generative AI solutions. Operating margin expansion and robust cash flow highlight operational efficiency. Revenue growth decelerated slightly from 15% YoY in Q1 2024 to 12%, but constant currency growth remains solid at 14%.
reports.further_direction: Cloud growth momentum and AI investments are key drivers. Continued focus on margin expansion and free cash flow generation. Risks include foreign exchange volatility, competition, and macroeconomic uncertainties as noted in forward-looking statements.

reports.title: 2025q1-alphabet-earnings-slides.pdf
reports.summary: Alphabet's Q1 2025 earnings show solid revenue growth, improved operating margins, and strong free cash flow. Google Cloud profitability increased significantly. Forward-looking statements caution about risks, but no specific guidance provided.
reports.overview: Revenue and operating income grew year-over-year, with operating margin expanding to 33.9% from 31.6%. Google Cloud operating margin jumped to 17.8% from 9.4%. Free cash flow remained robust, with TTM free cash flow of $39.1 billion. Capital expenditures increased. The company maintains a strong balance sheet.
reports.further_direction: No explicit guidance given; typical risk factors cited. Investors should monitor revenue growth trajectory, margin sustainability, cloud momentum, and capex efficiency. Macroeconomic and competitive risks remain.

reports.title: 2025q2-alphabet-earnings-release.pdf
reports.summary: Alphabet reported Q2 2025 revenues of $96.4 billion, up 14% YoY (13% constant currency). Operating income rose 14% to $31.3 billion, with an operating margin of 32.4%. Google Cloud revenues surged 32% to $13.6 billion, driven by GCP and AI solutions. Free cash flow improved to $24.8 billion for the quarter. EPS grew to $2.31 from $1.89.
reports.overview: The quarter showcased robust momentum across all segments, with double-digit growth in Search, YouTube, subscriptions, and Cloud. AI integration is positively impacting the business, evidenced by strong performance of AI Overviews and AI Mode. Operating margins benefited from revenue growth and cost efficiencies, partially offset by a legal settlement charge. Capital expenditures increased to support Cloud and AI infrastructure.
reports.further_direction: Alphabet plans to increase investment in AI and Cloud infrastructure given strong demand. Risks include legal settlements, currency fluctuations, and competitive pressures. Continued focus on cost discipline and innovation is expected to sustain growth, though margin expansion may moderate due to higher capex.
